title: The war in Iran will have knock-on effects for the steel markets in the upcoming months
section_path: The war in Iran will have knock-on effects for the steel markets in the upcoming months
section_path_text: The war in Iran will have knock-on effects for the steel markets in the upcoming months
summary:
Main
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-03-26 163723.png | assets/2026-03-26_the-war-in-iran-will-have-knock-on-effects-for-the-steel-markets-in-the-upcoming_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_0f9151237aa9.png
Key Takeaways
● Global steel flows remain flat in February. ● Flows from China have increased by 6%. ● Flows from outside of China have decreased by 4%.

Key Takeaways
● Global steel flows remain flat in February. ● Flows from China have increased by 6%.

children.title: China's steel exports can withstand the war in Iran
section_path: China's steel exports can withstand the war in Iran
children.section_path_text: China's steel exports can withstand the war in Iran
children.summary: Against this backdrop, Chinese steel exports are expected to remain resilient. The ongoing structural shift toward Southeast Asia, where demand is underpinned by construction-led growth and trade barriers are relatively limited, provides a stable outlet for surplus Chinese production.
